codeReview.summary: Observation storage and stale-row cleanup are implemented, but the reviewed commit does not expose stable row identity/read-only metadata through the Claude platform/detail query surface, so duplicate rows still collapse in the existing UI flow and marketplace rows still behave like manageable installs.

issues.description: Claude observation rows are returned through the old `SkillForAgent` contract without `row_id`/source/read-only metadata, while the existing detail/list consumers still key by `skill.id`; duplicate user+marketplace rows therefore cannot stay distinct through list/detail, and marketplace rows still inherit normal install/manage affordances. Evidence also involves `src-tauri/src/commands/skills.rs` around line 115 and `src/pages/PlatformView.tsx` around line 173.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `followedProcedure` true even though the assigned skill requires a stable row identity that survives list-to-detail flow and a backend contract update when payloads change; this feature stored `row_id` internally but did not expose it in the reviewed API surface.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill: `.factory/skills/claude-scan-backend-worker/SKILL.md` step 4/5; reviewed commit: `src-tauri/src/db.rs` around lines 967-1009 and `src-tauri/src/commands/skills.rs` around lines 115-148.

summary: Fail: storage/query groundwork landed, but the reviewed feature stops short of the row-aware platform/detail contract needed to keep duplicate Claude rows distinct and marketplace rows read-only in the live app flow.
